title: Submit a feedback or grievance entry
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to submit a feedback or grievance-related entry, so that my concern is formally captured and routed for role-based review.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated Employee (User), when I open the Feedback and Grievance module, then I can create a new feedback or grievance-related entry.; Given I am creating an entry, when I fill in the entry, then I can capture the required details of my feedback or grievance concern.; Given I complete the required details, when I submit the entry, then the entry is recorded in the system and enters the role-based review workflow.; Given required details are missing, when I attempt to submit, then submission is blocked and I am prompted to complete the mandatory fields.; Given I have submitted an entry, when I revisit the module, then I can see the entry I raised along with its current status.
source: FR 6.19.1 (submission of feedback and grievance-related entries with role-based review)

title: Role-based review of submitted entries
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want submitted feedback and grievance entries routed to me for review based on my role, so that the right authorized reviewer handles each entry.
acceptance_criteria: Given an entry has been submitted, when I access the module in a review role, then I can view the entries assigned for my review.; Given I hold a reviewer role, when I open an entry, then I can review its details as part of the role-based review workflow.; Given I hold a reviewer role, when I act on an entry, then only the review actions permitted for my role are available to me.; Given a user does not hold a review role, when they attempt to review an entry, then the review actions are not available to them.; Given multiple reviewers exist, when entries are routed, then each entry is directed to reviewers authorized for that entry's scope.
source: FR 6.19.1 (role-based review)

title: Restrict access to grievance information
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want access to grievance-related information restricted to authorized personnel, so that sensitive concerns remain confidential.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user is not authorized, when they attempt to view grievance-related information, then access is denied.; Given a user is authorized, when they access grievance-related information, then only the entries within their authorization scope are visible.; Given grievance data exists, when access is granted, then it is limited strictly to authorized personnel.; Given an unauthorized user attempts access, when the attempt occurs, then it is prevented and, where applicable, logged for auditability.
source: FR 6.19.2 (access restricted to authorized personnel)

title: Track the status of an entry
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to track the status of my submitted feedback or grievance entry, so that I know how it is progressing.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have submitted an entry, when I view it, then its current status is displayed.; Given the status of an entry changes, when I revisit the entry, then the updated status is reflected.; Given I have multiple entries, when I open the module, then I can see the status of each of my entries.
source: FR 6.19.3 (status tracking)

title: Update status during review
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to update the status of a feedback or grievance entry as I review it, so that its progress is accurately reflected.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authorized reviewer, when I review an entry, then I can update its status.; Given I update an entry's status, when the change is saved, then the new status is recorded and visible to the relevant parties, including the submitter.; Given I am not an authorized reviewer, when I attempt to change an entry's status, then the action is not permitted.; Given a status change occurs, when it is saved, then it is captured in the entry's audit trail.
source: FR 6.19.3 (status tracking)

title: Maintain an audit trail
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the module to maintain an audit trail of feedback and grievance activity, so that actions on each entry are traceable and accountable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an entry is submitted, reviewed, or updated, when the action occurs, then it is recorded for auditability.; Given an action is recorded, when it is captured, then the audit record identifies the action taken and the associated context needed for accountability.; Given an audit trail exists, when an authorized user inspects an entry, then the recorded history of actions on that entry is available.; Given a user is not authorized, when they attempt to view the audit trail, then access is denied.
source: FR 6.19.3 (auditability)

title: Scope module to Phase 1 needs
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the module scoped to Phase 1 feedback and grievance tracking needs, so that current requirements are met while broader case-management is deferred to a future phase.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Phase 1 release, when the module is used, then it supports feedback and grievance tracking without full case-management workflows.; Given broader case-management capabilities are planned for a future phase, when Phase 1 scope is defined, then only feedback and grievance tracking features are included.; Given the module is delivered, when Phase 1 capabilities are enabled, then submission, role-based review, access restriction, status tracking, and auditability are supported as the in-scope feature set.
source: FR 6.19.4 (Phase 1 scope; case-management deferred to future phase)

title: Submit an entry on behalf of a non-user employee
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to submit a feedback or grievance-related entry on behalf of an Employee (Non-User), so that employees without system access can still have their concerns formally captured and reviewed.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Employee (Non-User) has no system access, when they raise a concern to me, then I can create a feedback or grievance-related entry on their behalf.; Given I create an entry on behalf of a non-user employee, when I record it, then the entry captures the concerned employee's identity and required details.; Given the entry is submitted, when it is recorded, then it enters the same role-based review workflow as directly submitted entries; Given the entry exists, when it is inspected, then the audit trail reflects that it was submitted on behalf of the non-user employee.
source: FR 6.19.1 (submission of feedback and grievance-related entries; Employee (Non-User) coverage)

title: Scoped review and oversight across group companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want role-based review and access to feedback and grievance entries limited to the companies within my group, so that oversight respects organizational and confidentiality boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Given entries exist across multiple companies, when I access the module, then I can view and review only the entries belonging to companies within my group scope.; Given an entry is outside my group scope, when I attempt to access it, then access is denied.; Given I hold a reviewer role, when I review entries within my scope, then role-based review and status update actions are available to me for those entries.; Given I access grievance information, when it is displayed, then it remains restricted to authorized personnel within my authorization scope.
source: FR 6.19.1, FR 6.19.2 (role-based review; access restricted to authorized personnel)

title: Portfolio-level access and configuration
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want authorized, scope-limited access to feedback and grievance tracking across my portfolio, so that I can oversee the process and ensure the module is available and correctly restricted for each company.
acceptance_criteria: Given the module is provisioned, when I configure it for companies in my portfolio, then feedback and grievance tracking, role-based review, and access restrictions are enabled per company.; Given entries exist across my portfolio, when I access grievance-related information, then only entries within my authorized portfolio scope are visible.; Given a user is unauthorized, when access controls are applied, then grievance information remains restricted to authorized personnel.; Given oversight is required, when I inspect activity, then auditability and status tracking are available within my authorization scope.
source: FR 6.19.1, FR 6.19.2, FR 6.19.3 (role-based review; access restriction; auditability and status tracking)

title: Confidential handling of my grievance
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want my grievance-related information handled confidentially and shared only with authorized reviewers, so that I can raise sensitive concerns without exposure.
acceptance_criteria: Given I submit a grievance-related entry, when it is stored, then its information is restricted to authorized personnel only.; Given other employees do not have authorization, when they attempt to view my entry, then access is denied.; Given I view my own entry, when I open it, then I can see my submission and its current status.; Given confidentiality applies, when my entry is reviewed, then only authorized reviewers within scope can access its details.
source: FR 6.19.2 (access restricted to authorized personnel); FR 6.19.3 (status tracking)

title: Canonical tenant-scoped data model with status history
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want feedback and grievance entries persisted in a canonical, tenant-scoped data model that retains full status history, so that records are isolated per tenant and their progression is reconstructable over time.
acceptance_criteria: Given an entry is created, when it is persisted, then it is stored against the owning tenant with tenant-scoped isolation (RLS) so that no cross-tenant read is possible.; Given an entry's status changes over time, when each change is written, then the effective-dated history is retained rather than overwritten, so the full progression can be reconstructed.; Given an entry references a submitter or concerned employee, when it is stored, then the relationship to that person record is preserved with integrity.; Given a query runs for an unauthorized tenant, when access is evaluated at the data layer, then no rows outside the caller's tenant scope are returned.
source: FR 6.19.2, FR 6.19.3 (L1 domain + data: tenant-scoped persistence, status history, integrity)

title: Enable and govern the module per tenant via config
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the Feedback and Grievance module governed by per-tenant configuration, so that its availability and scope can be turned on or adjusted for my company without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given the module supports per-tenant toggling, when I enable it for my company, then employees in that company gain access to submission and tracking, and when disabled the module is not available.; Given configuration is versioned and effective-dated, when I change a module setting, then the change takes effect from its effective date and prior settings remain recorded.; Given only authorized administrators may change configuration, when a non-authorized user attempts a config change, then the change is blocked.; Given the module is scoped to Phase 1, when configuration is applied, then only in-scope feedback and grievance tracking capabilities are exposed.
source: FR 6.19.1, FR 6.19.4 (L2 config: per-tenant module toggle, versioned/effective-dated)

title: Configure entry categories and submission form schema
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure the categories and the submission form fields (including custom fields) for feedback and grievance entries, so that captured details match my company's needs without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold configuration rights, when I define entry categories/types and the submission field schema, then those definitions are saved as governed, versioned configuration for my tenant.; Given a field is marked mandatory in config, when an employee submits an entry, then that field is enforced as required at submission.; Given I add or change a custom field, when the change is effective-dated, then new submissions use the updated schema while existing entries retain the schema under which they were captured.; Given a non-authorized user attempts to change the schema, when they try, then the change is not permitted.
source: FR 6.19.1 (L2 config: category and UDF/field schema definitions)

title: Configure reviewer routing and access rules
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure which roles review which entries and who may access grievance information, so that role-based review and access restriction are driven by governed configuration rather than hard-coded rules.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold configuration rights, when I define the reviewer/approver routing for entry types, then that routing is saved as governed configuration the workflow engine will read.; Given I define authorized-personnel access rules, when they are saved, then grievance visibility is limited to the roles named in that configuration.; Given the routing configuration is versioned and effective-dated, when I change it, then future submissions route per the new configuration while prior routing history remains recorded.; Given a non-authorized user attempts to change routing or access rules, when they try, then the change is blocked.
source: FR 6.19.1, FR 6.19.2 (L2 config: approver graph and access rules)

title: Workflow engine routes entries per configured graph
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared workflow engine to route each submitted entry to reviewers according to the configured approver graph, so that role-based review is executed consistently by the engine.
acceptance_criteria: Given a reviewer routing graph is configured for a tenant, when an entry is submitted, then the workflow engine evaluates that configuration and assigns the entry to the authorized reviewer role(s).; Given a reviewer acts on an entry, when the engine advances the workflow, then the entry transitions to the next configured review state.; Given routing configuration differs between tenants, when entries are submitted, then each tenant's entries are routed per its own configuration without cross-tenant leakage.; Given no matching reviewer is configured, when the engine evaluates routing, then the entry is held/flagged rather than routed to an unauthorized party.
source: FR 6.19.1 (L3 engine: workflow/approval executes configured routing)

title: Templated notifications on submission and status change
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want the notification engine to send templated notifications when my entry is submitted and when its status changes, so that I and the reviewers stay informed without manual updates.
acceptance_criteria: Given I submit an entry, when it is recorded, then the notification engine issues a submission acknowledgement using the configured template.; Given an authorized reviewer changes an entry's status, when the change is saved, then the notification engine notifies the submitter (and relevant reviewers) using the configured status-change template.; Given a notification is sent, when it is generated, then it respects confidentiality and reveals grievance details only to authorized recipients.; Given notification templates are governed configuration, when a template is updated, then subsequent notifications use the updated template.
source: FR 6.19.3 (L3 engine: notification/template on status change)

title: Dynamic submission form rendered from configured schema
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want the submission form to be rendered dynamically from the configured field schema, so that I only see the categories and fields my company has defined.
acceptance_criteria: Given a submission field schema is configured for my tenant, when I open the submission form, then the forms engine renders the fields and categories from that configuration.; Given a field is configured as mandatory, when I attempt to submit without it, then the forms engine enforces the requirement and blocks submission.; Given the schema is updated by an administrator, when I next open the form, then the rendered form reflects the updated schema without any code change.; Given a field has configured validation, when I enter an invalid value, then the forms engine surfaces the validation error before submission.
source: FR 6.19.1 (L3 engine: forms/dynamic-fields renders configured schema)

title: Reviewer worklist with search and filtering
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven worklist screen to search, filter, and open the feedback and grievance entries assigned to me, so that I can efficiently manage my review queue.
acceptance_criteria: Given entries are routed to me for review, when I open the module, then I see a worklist grid of entries within my authorization scope.; Given the worklist has many entries, when I search or filter by attributes such as status or category, then the grid updates to show matching entries only.; Given I select an entry from the worklist, when I open it, then its detail view is displayed with the actions permitted for my role.; Given an entry is outside my authorization scope, when the worklist is rendered, then it is not shown to me.
source: FR 6.19.1, FR 6.19.2, FR 6.19.3 (L4 presentation: worklist grid, search/navigation)

title: Submit an anonymous feedback or grievance
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to submit a feedback or grievance entry anonymously, so that I can raise sensitive concerns without my identity being disclosed.
acceptance_criteria: Given anonymous feedback/grievance is enabled for my company, when I create an entry, then I can choose to submit it anonymously.; Given I submit anonymously, when the entry is recorded, then my identity is withheld from receivers and reviewers while the concern is still captured and routed.; Given anonymous support is disabled, when I create an entry, then the anonymous option is not offered and entries carry my identity.; Given I submitted anonymously, when I revisit the module, then I can still track the entry's status through an anonymous reference without exposing my identity.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Do you support Anonymous Feedback / Grievance?)

title: Enable and govern anonymous feedback support
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to turn anonymous feedback/grievance support on or off, so that anonymous submissions are only accepted when my company chooses to allow them.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the receiver configuration screen, when I set "Do you support Anonymous Feedback / Grievance?" to Yes, then the anonymous submission option and anonymous receiver settings become applicable.; Given I set anonymous support to No, when employees create entries, then the anonymous option is not available.; Given the setting is governed configuration, when I change it and save, then the new setting takes effect for subsequent submissions.; Given a non-authorized user attempts to change the anonymous setting, when they try, then the change is blocked.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Do you support Anonymous Feedback / Grievance?)

title: Configure separate receiver roles for anonymous and non-anonymous entries
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign different receiver roles for anonymous versus non-anonymous feedback/grievance, so that each submission type routes to the appropriate recipients.
acceptance_criteria: Given anonymous support is enabled, when I configure receivers, then I can assign one set of applicable roles as Non-Anonymous receivers and a separate set as Anonymous receivers.; Given an anonymous entry is submitted, when it is routed, then it goes only to the configured anonymous receiver roles.; Given a non-anonymous entry is submitted, when it is routed, then it goes to the configured non-anonymous receiver roles.; Given a non-authorized user attempts to change receiver roles, when they try, then the change is blocked.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Non Anonymous / Anonymous Receiver(s) applicable roles)

title: Assign receiver roles via available/selected dual-list picker
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to move roles between an available list and a selected list, so that I can precisely control which roles are feedback/grievance receivers.
acceptance_criteria: Given the receiver role picker is shown, when I use the move-one and move-all controls, then roles transfer between the available and selected lists accordingly.; Given I have moved roles into the selected list, when I save, then only the selected roles are configured as receivers.; Given I remove a role from the selected list, when I save, then that role no longer receives feedback/grievance entries.; Given a large role catalog exists, when I manage the lists, then I can add or remove individual roles or all roles at once.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (role dual-list controls >> > < <<)

title: Configure an approver reminder SLA
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to set how many days after receiving an entry the approver is reminded, so that approvers act on feedback/grievance in a timely manner.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring receivers, when I enter a value for "Reminder to the approver after" days, then the approver is reminded that many days after receiving the entry if no action has been taken.; Given the reminder value is saved, when the configured days elapse without approver action, then a reminder is issued to the approver.; Given the approver acts before the SLA elapses, when the days pass, then no redundant reminder is sent.; Given a non-authorized user attempts to change the SLA, when they try, then the change is blocked.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Reminder to the approver after N days)

title: Configure escalation to the feedback coordinator
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want stalled entries escalated to the feedback/grievance coordinator after a configured number of days of approver inaction, so that unresolved concerns are not left unattended.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring receivers, when I enter a value for "Reminder to feedback coordinator after" days, then the coordinator is reminded that many days after the entry is received if the approver has not acted.; Given the approver has not acted within the configured window, when the days elapse, then the entry is escalated to the feedback/grievance coordinator.; Given the approver acts within the window, when the days pass, then no escalation to the coordinator is triggered.; Given the escalation SLA is governed configuration, when I change it and save, then future entries use the updated threshold.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Reminder to feedback coordinator after N days if approver has not acted)

title: Designate a feedback/grievance coordinator
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to designate a feedback/grievance coordinator, so that there is an accountable owner for oversight and escalation of stalled entries.
acceptance_criteria: Given the receiver configuration screen, when I assign a Feedback / Grievance Coordinator, then that role is recorded as the escalation and oversight owner.; Given a coordinator is designated, when an entry is escalated, then it is directed to the designated coordinator.; Given no coordinator is designated but escalation is configured, when I attempt to save, then I am prompted to designate a coordinator.; Given the coordinator assignment changes, when I save, then subsequent escalations route to the newly designated coordinator.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Feedback / Grievance Coordinator)

title: Automated reminder and escalation notifications driven by SLA
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the system to automatically issue approver reminders and coordinator escalations based on the configured day thresholds, so that timeliness is enforced without manual monitoring.
acceptance_criteria: Given approver and coordinator SLA thresholds are configured, when an entry ages past a threshold without the required action, then the system automatically issues the corresponding reminder or escalation.; Given a reminder is issued, when it is generated, then it identifies the entry and the pending action for the recipient while respecting confidentiality.; Given an anonymous entry triggers a reminder, when it is sent, then the submitter's identity remains withheld.; Given the action is completed, when the entry advances, then further scheduled reminders for that stage are stopped.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (approver reminder + coordinator escalation SLAs)

title: Manage feedback/grievance email templates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to manage the email templates used for feedback/grievance notifications, so that acknowledgements, reminders, and escalations use approved, consistent wording.
acceptance_criteria: Given the feedback/grievance configuration has an Email Templates section, when I open it, then I can view and edit the templates for submission acknowledgement, approver reminder, and coordinator escalation.; Given I edit a template and save, when the corresponding event next occurs, then the notification uses the updated template.; Given templates are governed configuration, when I change one, then the change is recorded and applies to subsequent notifications only.; Given a non-authorized user attempts to edit a template, when they try, then the change is blocked.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Email Templates tab)

title: Progress feedback/grievance setup as a guided multi-step configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure the module through a guided Setup then Receivers then Email Templates flow with Save and Save & Next, so that I can complete the setup in the correct order without missing steps.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Setup step, when I enable the module and click Save & Next, then the setting is saved and I advance to the Feedback / Grievance Receivers step.; Given I am on the Receivers step, when I complete receiver and SLA settings and click Save & Next, then I advance to the Email Templates step.; Given I click Save on any step, when the save succeeds, then my changes on that step persist without advancing.; Given I click Cancel, when I confirm, then my unsaved changes on the current step are discarded.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Setup and Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Save / Save & Next multi-step flow)

title: Anonymous submitter identity withheld from receivers
user_story: As an Employee (User) submitting anonymously, I want my identity concealed from receivers and reviewers, so that I am protected from retaliation while my concern is still actioned.
acceptance_criteria: Given I submit an anonymous entry, when a receiver or reviewer opens it, then the entry content is visible but my identity is not disclosed.; Given the entry is escalated to the coordinator, when they view it, then it remains anonymized.; Given notifications are sent for an anonymous entry, when they are generated, then they omit any identifying details of the submitter.; Given I track my anonymous entry, when I open it, then I can see its status without my identity being exposed to others.
source: Kensium: Feedback - Grievance > Feedback / Grievance Receivers (Anonymous Feedback / Grievance receivers)
